title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox defeated Yankees 4-1 for their third consecutive win against the A.L.-leading team.
Masataka Yoshida and Anthony Seigler hit early home runs against Yankees' pitcher Gerrit Cole.
Jake Bennett pitched 6.1 innings, allowing three hits and earning his second career win.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded forward Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche, acquiring Ivan Ivan in return.
Fabian Lysell, the Bruins' 2021 first-round pick, appeared in 12 NHL games, scoring one goal and two assists.
Ivan Ivan played 66 games for the Colorado Eagles in AHL last season, recording 11 goals and 15 assists.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 at Gillette Stadium, enhancing their reputation as the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup.
Key players for France included Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé, both contributing to the team's dynamic attacking style.
Although Norway had a higher expected goals metric than France, the French team's efficient offense secured a decisive victory.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich Middle High School is under investigation following a student's alleged threat against two classmates reported via a safety app.
The investigation involves Sandwich police and the Cape & Islands District Attorney's Office; the student involved is not attending school.
School officials informed the families of those targeted and are collaborating with authorities to ensure safety for all students.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, convicted of first-degree murder for 2015 Dorchester home invasion, faces life without parole.
Arrington and accomplices mistakenly entered Richard Long's apartment, murdering him and injuring his fiancée.
Location data from Arrington's iPhone used as evidence in retrial leading to a murder conviction.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' features 18 New England contestants with 12 from Massachusetts, starting June 29.
Taylor Johnson and Addy Herman are notable New England contestants, training at Vitality Obstacle Fitness in Fall River.
The new season includes a triple-lane race format called Tripleheader, increasing competition intensity and excitement.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish soccer fans flooded Boston for the World Cup, bringing kilts and a festive atmosphere to the city.
Scottish men assured their loyalty, claiming they wouldn't cheat while exploring potential romances during their stay in Boston.
Boston's Love Letters newsletter inquired about locals finding romance with Scottish visitors during the World Cup event.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Shaolin Soccer" combines soccer with Shaolin kung fu, offering a sports comedy available on Paramount+, Tubi, and The Roku Channel.
"Diamantino" is a political satire featuring a Ronaldo-like character, streaming on Kanopy and Hoopla.
"United Passions", considered a historically poor film, grossed $918 on its opening weekend, available on Tubi and The Roku Channel.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Trump administration is considering deregulating maritime slow zones, potentially affecting North Atlantic right whale safety.
North Atlantic right whales, numbering about 380 in 2024, are severely endangered and benefit from reduced vessel strikes due to current rules.
Advocates argue advanced whale-detection technology could replace slow zones, but conservationists warn this may threaten whale recovery efforts.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings and struck out seven, allowing just one hit against the Yankees at Fenway.
Tolle dealt with an illness but maintained a no-hit bid into the sixth inning in the Red Sox's 6-1 victory.
Tolle holds a 2.78 ERA this season and has excelled against the Yankees with 18 strikeouts in two starts.
